73593.subject: What comes next for Donald Trump and Iran?
73593.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODd9AAcAAAAAAdoAvj6QMRfkn00hrMWiX3eDO5hQVaihk-IpQzs2ziVpZ0bWDHLdPDl7wdzKyli15DmfCt7Li96Af4scEBBBocKm5Laml0-qMfFsi1NwmKhsaA 

May 25th 2026

 

 
 
 

 The Economist Today
 


 Our best journalism, handpicked each day
 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODd9AAcAAAAAAdoAvj6Rbkn_HQZWCWRIfHSIyz4Zg9DNySudOdX9ZY5BhHhWVGKUPcn3FrnV2VRr3JBJq_psAwnzmAsqpq7g_-Cj-xbyHF2Xn147SHIf9lb9hg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODd9AAcAAAAAAdoAvj6S7RV8yFCOQtoTOXTAsltU8TcgIld77gL_153es-Z-7KQ8XDVYFgjyUZiAxA0jWDVuTGZ_bHTwOSWN8qugU7kyCgDYVc3GlKgp13SLhA 



 
 

Lane Greene

Senior digital editor



 Where is the conflict with Iran headed? Nobody seems to know, much less Donald Trump. The president touted a deal on Saturday, seemed to walk it back on Sunday, and has been bashing critics (including Republicans who have cavilled) as “losers”. Iran, meanwhile, wants a deal but still seems to think time is on its side. At this point, an agreement to keep talking—a deal to make a deal—is the 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4ODd9AAcAAAAAAdoAvj6TzqWavc4SncZZe9x-6muE00p-VllI3jQ8vz280lhxLBoENCmLVCmiyRnEs_mtyzNOg0zT2pcXncmJiwv9leGe2uMdQVrNwuCm 
best-case scenario .

AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-may-25-oleh-bezuhlyi-6za2f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BaK2DGGzvQSW5eXaoAVavMQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2cmIuLWIiHCYg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mplfotl5~65-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mplfotl5-65
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment has dipped into "Extreme Fear" despite landmark institutional breakthroughs. Strategic Bitcoin disclosures from SpaceX and major regulatory shifts from the White House define a high-stakes 24 hours for the global digital asset ecosystem. 🚀SpaceX Discloses 18,712 BTC Holdings in New SEC Filing In a major filing ahead of its anticipated IPO, SpaceX revealed it holds over 18,700 Bitcoin, surpassing Tesla’s current reserves. The disclosure positions the aerospace giant as one of the world's most significant corporate BTC treasury holders. Read full story 🚫Indonesia Blocks Polymarket Amid National Gambling Crackdown Indonesia’s Ministry of Communication and Digital has restricted access to the decentralized prediction platform Polymarket, classifying it as illegal online gambling. The move follows a viral contract speculating on the country’s presidency. See details 🏛️Trump Issues Executive Order to Streamline Crypto Banking Access A new executive order from Donald Trump directs federal regulators to review and open federal payment rails to compliant digital asset firms. The order mandates a 90-day review period to identify and remove barriers to institutional banking partnerships. Read full story 🌍Tether Partners with Georgia for GELT Stablecoin Launch Tether has announced the launch of "GELT," a stablecoin pegged to the Georgian lari, with full backing from the Georgian government. The project aims to integrate blockchain-based programmable payments into the nation’s core financial infrastructure. See details 🛡️Ethereum Upgrade "Hegota" to Feature Native Protocol Privacy Developers have proposed EIP-8182 for Ethereum’s upcoming Hegota upgrade, introducing native private transfers for ETH and ERC-20 tokens. The proposal seeks to solve liquidity fragmentation by integrating a shared protocol-managed shielded pool. Read full story 🤝SWIFT Directory Formally Recognizes Ripple Treasury Integration Ripple Treasury has been added to SWIFT’s Business Solutions Providers Directory as a certified partner for ISO 20022 compliance. This certification marks a pivotal moment for the institutional adoption of Ripple’s liquidity solutions within traditional banking. Read full story 📉Analysts Signal Market Rotation Despite Record Bitcoin ETF Outflows While U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $1.26 billion in

73590.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will sell less ETH
73590.body:
Satoshi-era bitcoin whale moves over $200 million in BTC to FalconX, Cumberland | Tether plans GELT stablecoin launch with support from Georgian government | Indonesia blocks Polymarket amid crackdown on online betting | 'Institutional bid hasn't disappeared': Analysts say bitcoin cooldown, spot ETF outflows signal rotation, not exit |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Naga Avan-Nomayo

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Monday! It may be a holiday across the U.S. and in several other countries, but we're still here to keep you updated on the industry that never sleeps.

In today's newsletter, *Vitalik Buterin* says the *Ethereum Foundation* will sell less *ETH* , a *Satoshi* -era *bitcoin* whale moves over $200 million in *BTC* , *Tether* plans to launch a *GELT* stablecoin with *Georgian* government support, and more.

Meanwhile, a newly introduced ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402264/new-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-bill-drops-btc-purchase-target-adds-lockup?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *strategic bitcoin reserve* bill dropped its 1 million BTC purchase target and added a 20-year lockup period.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*👀 Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will be a 'smaller ship,' sell less ETH amid researcher exodus*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402435/vitalik-buterin-says-ethereum-foundation-will-be-a-smaller-ship-sell-less-eth-amid-researcher-exodus?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Vitalik Buterin* said the *Ethereum Foundation* plans to become a "smaller ship" focused on longevity and *Ethereum's* censorship resistance, privacy, and security properties rather than broader ecosystem expansion.

* Buterin framed the post as his personal view rather than an official Ethereum Foundation position while defending the nonprofit's restructuring amid a wave of high-profile departures.
* The Ethereum co-founder added that the EF will sell less *ETH* going forward and emphasized the organization now controls just roughly 0.16% of the circulating *ether* supply.
* Buterin outlined three technical priorities for Ethereum's next phase: AI-assisted formal verification, resilient consensus design, and minimizing intermediaries across the network stack.
* He rejecte

73589.subject: 4 expensive misconceptions about AI agents
73589.body:
AI Frontier

Автор: Steve Nouri


Автор: Steve Nouri

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/4-expensive-misconceptions-ai-agents-steve-nouri-ffevc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BtHAQKLHhRqS7Of%2BIHMDxAA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0YzL-6u9dZCYg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mple0s7p~9g-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mple0s7p-9g
  
Most enterprises are not wasting millions on AI agents because agents are bad. They are wasting millions because they misunderstand what agents are actually for. I keep seeing the same pattern. A team gets excited about agents. They run a pilot. The demo looks impressive. The board likes it. The vendor says it can scale. The internal team starts imagining AI employees across the business. Then reality hits. The agent works in a controlled environment, but struggles inside the actual organization. Not because the model is stupid. Not because the team is lazy. Not because AI is overhyped. But because the enterprise is building agents on top of the wrong assumptions. Here are four misconceptions I think are costing companies serious money. Why is your AI experiment still gathering dust? Everyone was excited about the pilot. Seven months later, nothing had changed. Seen this happen before? Without solid data foundations underneath, even brilliant AI ideas collapse. That’s the part many AI conversations miss. Senior leaders from Yahoo, Mercedes-Benz, Regeneron and AWS have cracked the code on moving from isolated pilots to production-scale AI that delivers results. Five senior executives from pharma, automotive, media and cloud share practical strategies for proving AI impact early, accelerating adoption, and embedding AI across your entire enterprise. If your AI projects keep stalling, this one’s for you. Watch now 1. “We need more data.” This one sounds right. And to be fair, sometimes it is right. Most large companies already have more data than they know what to do with. Data warehouses. Dashboards. Documents. Data lakes. CRM records. ERP systems. Tickets. Logs. Policies. Meeting notes. Customer histories. Knowledge bases. The problem is not simply that the data does not exist. The problem is that the data does not explain how the business actually works. A customer record is data. Knowing why this customer gets special treatment is context. A policy document is data. Knowing which clause applies to this specific case is context. A dashboard is data. Knowing what decision it should trigger is context. A ticket history is data. Knowing which past incident is truly similar is context. A process map is data. Knowing the informal workaround everyone uses because the official process is broken is context. This is the mistake. Enterprises think AI needs mor
